title: signatures.rs: SignatureParts fields are owned Strings, should be &str
description:
LOCATION: src/signatures.rs lines 105-109, 114-134

FINDING: SignatureParts { key_id: String, headers: String, signature: String } is produced by parse_signature_header(header: &str) and consumed immediately in verify_signature() in the same call chain. All three fields are substrings of the input header value. They could be &str slices borrowed from the input, eliminating three String allocations per inbound request.

This is the same class of issue as the sig_header/digest_header owned String finding from pass 1, applied to the struct itself.

IMPACT: Three String allocations per verified inbox request. With signatures already noted as a hot path (pass 1 flagged PEM re-parsing), the struct fields are the next allocation in that path.
close_reason: Documented: ponytail comment explaining owned Strings needed for return value lifetime
